entities.name: STRIGOLACTONES
entities.description: Plant hormones that act as germination stimulants for parasitic plants and regulate various plant growth and stress responses

entities.name: STRIGA LUTEA
entities.description: A parasitic plant species whose germination is stimulated by strigolactones

entities.name: PHELIPANCHE
entities.description: A genus of parasitic plants affected by strigolactones

entities.name: OROBANCHE
entities.description: A genus of parasitic plants affected by strigolactones

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A major cereal crop in which strigolactones are reported

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A major cereal crop in which strigolactones are reported

entities.name: HOST-PARASITE INTERACTION
entities.description: The biological interaction between a host plant and a parasitic plant

entities.name: ROOT AND SHOOT ARCHITECTURE
entities.description: The physical organization and growth patterns of plant roots and shoots

entities.name: PATHOGEN INTERACTION
entities.description: The process of interaction between a plant and a pathogen

entities.name: PLANT DEFENSE MECHANISM
entities.description: The biological processes used by plants to protect themselves from pests and diseases

entities.name: NUTRITION UPTAKE
entities.description: The process by which plants absorb nutrients from the environment

entities.name: ABIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand non-living environmental stressors

entities.name: BIOTIC STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand living environmental stressors

entities.name: PHYTOHORMONES
entities.description: Chemical messengers in plants that regulate growth and development

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The process of enhancing the genetic makeup of crops for better yield or traits

entities.name: COMMERCIAL AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The large-scale production of crops for sale
